name: Colours of MAATI - Handpainted stories from Madhubani - Jun 05
description: Concept Note: At MAATI – The Crafts School, we see craft as more than heritage. It is a language of material intelligence, cultural memory, and contemporary possibility. Through artisan-led learning, design mentorship, and enterprise development in the heart of Mithila, MAATI works alongside over 150 women artisans, ensuring the craft continues to evolve with dignity, relevance, and economic independence. We invite you to discover a curated and designed presentation of textile collections from Mithila, thoughtfully designed for contemporary homes, spaces, and collectors. Day One | 5 June 2026 An exclusive, invitation-only preview for architects, interior designers, hospitality partners, retailers, collectors, and institutional buyers—offering an opportunity to explore collections closely, discuss bespoke requirements, and initiate meaningful collaborations. Day Two | 6 June 2026 Open to all, the showcase welcomes those who value thoughtful making—to discover handcrafted collections, acquire one-of-a-kind pieces, and place custom-made orders rooted in material authenticity. More than an exhibition, this is a space for dialogue, discovery, and exchange—where craft is experienced not as heritage alone, but as a language for contemporary life. Contact number for event related enquiries - +91-8140037301 The roads leading up to Sabha are undergoing maintenance, please plan to arrive early and avail of public transport.  Parking is not available at the venue.

name: Organic World Fusion by Sarva ✨ - A meeting of East and West, where sound travels without borders.
Cast Your Jewel Workshop - Immersive, Hands on, Cast Your Own Silver Jewellery
description: Beginner Level | Timings - 11:00 - 5:00 pm Join us for an immersive hands-on workshop where you'll design and cast your own silver jewellery piece using the traditional sand casting process. Workshop Schedule 11:00 – 11:30 AM - Introduction & sand casting demonstration by the instructor 11:30 AM – 01:00 PM - Design and carve your piece in wax and prepare the sand mould 01:00 – 02:00 PM - Lunch break 02:00 – 05:00 PM - Melt and pour silver into your mould, then finish your piece by sawing, filing, soldering and polishing. Cost – ₹5,500 per participant Silver cost is not included Age Requirement – 14 years & above Workshop Safety Guidelines • Wear closed-toe shoes • Avoid loose clothing and dangling jewellery • Tie back long hair securely • Follow instructor guidance carefully • Handle all tools and equipment mindfully • Stay aware of your surroundings while working Your safety is our priority — please adhere to these guidelines throughout the workshop.  To register please contact:  +91 8050782762
